chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Front Matter: Foreword, Preface, How to Use This Book
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the front matter of the IVD book. Establishes author
# credibility, the problem, the audience, the book's structure, and reading
# paths—before the reader enters Part I (Chapter 1).
#
# Cover & Title Page and Table of Contents are production artifacts (auto-generated
# or publisher-driven) and are not included in this intent. They are defined in
# book_system_intent.yaml front_matter but do not require a manuscript.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.front_matter
# Source:    research/best_selling_programming_books.md (sections 3.2, 4.1, 5, 6.1)
# Version: 1.2
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "front_matter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should the front matter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Establish why this book exists, who it is for, why the reader should trust the author, and how to read the book—before the reader enters Chapter 1."

  goal: |
    By the end of the front matter, the reader:
    1. Trusts the author: knows Leo's journey, expertise, and why he wrote this book
    2. Understands the problem at a glance: misalignment in human-AI collaboration
    3. Knows what they will learn, build, and validate
    4. Knows who this book is for (and who it is not for)
    5. Has a reading path: Practitioner, Leader, or Complete
    6. Knows where to find templates, appendices, and the code repository
    7. Feels compelled to start Chapter 1

    Front matter is the book's handshake. Pattern from research: Code Complete ("Why This Book"),
    Clean Code (preface), Pragmatic Programmer (foreword + "Why We Wrote This Book"). Establish
    problem and audience in the first 2 pages.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can state what the book is about in one sentence
    - Reader knows if this book is for them (developer, tech lead, AI-era builder)
    - Reader has chosen a reading path (Practitioner, Leader, or Complete)
    - Reader trusts the author's credibility (experience, evidence, journey)
    - Reader is ready to start Chapter 1 with context and motivation

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (front_matter)"
    - "Potential readers (first impression; buy/continue decision)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "foreword"
      title: "Foreword"
      required_content:
        - "Written by an industry leader (TBD); author solicits and provides context"
        - "The AI

- Reader is ready to start Chapter 1 with context and motivation

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (front_matter)"
    - "Potential readers (first impression; buy/continue decision)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "foreword"
      title: "Foreword"
      required_content:
        - "Written by an industry leader (TBD); author solicits and provides context"
        - "The AI revolution's missing piece: why intent matters more than code"
        - "Personal experience with IVD or the problem IVD solves"
        - "Endorsement: why this book matters now"
        - "Pattern: Code Complete (foreword by industry figure), Pragmatic Programmer (foreword)"
      notes: |
        The foreword is written by a third party, not the author. The intent here defines
        what the author should seek in the foreword contributor and what the foreword should cover.
        Manuscript will be a placeholder until the contributor is confirmed.

    - id: "preface"
      title: "Preface: Why This Book Exists"
      required_content:
        - "Establish the problem and who this is for in the first 2 pages (pattern: Code Complete, Clean Code)"
        - "The problem and its scale: misalignment in human-AI collaboration—many turns, hallucinations, wasted effort; the $10B+ problem (sourced or labeled 'estimated')"
        - "My journey: how I discovered intent as the primary artifact (author's personal story)"
        - "What you'll learn, build, and validate (book promise: theory → practice → proof → adoption)"
        - "How this book is different: evidence-based, tested with real projects, framework not opinion"
        - "Who should read this book: developers, tech leads, engineering managers, CS educators, anyone building with AI agents"
        - "Who this is NOT for: if you're not building with AI or don't care about verification"
        - "Quotable: one clear, memorable line that captures the book's thesis"
      writing_guidance: |
        This is the author's voice at its most personal. First person ("I") is appropriate here—
        the only place in the book where first person leads. Transition to second person ("you")
        when addressing the reader directly. Pattern: McConnell's preface is professional and direct;
        Clean Code's is brief and personal; Pragmatic's explains their motivation.

    - id: "how_to_use"
      title: "How to Use This Book"
      required_content:
        - "Three reading paths, each delivering value at exit (pattern: Code Complete):"
        - "  1. Practitioner Path: Part I → Part II. Focus on doing. Exit: building with IVD."
        - "  2. Leader Path: Part I → Part III → Part IV. Understand ROI and adoption. Exit: making the case and scaling."
        - "  3. Complete Path: Read sequentially. Full mastery and reference. Exit: expert."
        - "Book structure overview: four parts (Paradigm → Practice → Validation → Adoption)"
        - "Supporting resour

atic's explains their motivation.

    - id: "how_to_use"
      title: "How to Use This Book"
      required_content:
        - "Three reading paths, each delivering value at exit (pattern: Code Complete):"
        - "  1. Practitioner Path: Part I → Part II. Focus on doing. Exit: building with IVD."
        - "  2. Leader Path: Part I → Part III → Part IV. Understand ROI and adoption. Exit: making the case and scaling."
        - "  3. Complete Path: Read sequentially. Full mastery and reference. Exit: expert."
        - "Book structure overview: four parts (Paradigm → Practice → Validation → Adoption)"
        - "Supporting resources: code repository URL, templates, appendices (Quick Reference, Recipe Library)"
        - "Community and support: where to ask questions, share results, contribute"
        - "Dip-in use: appendices and recipes support lookup without sequential reading"
        - "Conventions used in this book: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, YAML for intent artifacts"
      writing_guidance: |
        Short and scannable. Use a clear list or table for the three paths. The reader should be
        able to pick their path in under 60 seconds. Pattern: Code Complete's "How to Use" is
        ~2 pages with a visual reading map.

  manuscript:
    files:
      - "foreword.md"
      - "preface.md"
      - "how_to_use.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Preface establishes problem and audience in first 2 pages"
      - "How to Use has three reading paths with clear entry/exit points"
      - "At least one quotable line in the preface"
      - "No chapter numbers (front matter is unnumbered)"
      - "Author's personal voice in preface (first person allowed here only)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (front matter does not have a Key Points section; these are the
# messages the front matter must convey)
# ==============================================================================

key_messages:
  - "This book teaches Intent-Verified Development: the framework for building with AI agents"
  - "The problem is real: many turns, hallucinations, misalignment—and it costs billions"
  - "The author built IVD from direct experience; this is evidence-based, not opinion"
  - "Three reading paths: Practitioner (build), Leader (adopt), Complete (master)"
  - "Templates, recipes, appendices, and a code repository support you beyond the book"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "problem_in_two_pages"
    requirement: "Preface establishes the problem and audience within the first 2 pages (~500–600 words)"
    source: "book_system_intent.front_matter.preface; research 3.2 (Code Complete, Clean Code pattern)"
    validation: "First 600 words of preface.md cover problem + audience"
    test: "Word count of preface.md through end of audience section ≤ 600; both 'problem' and 'audience' topics present"

  - name: "writing_style_front_matter"
    requirement: "Preface: first person ('I') for auth

# CONSTRAINTS
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "problem_in_two_pages"
    requirement: "Preface establishes the problem and audience within the first 2 pages (~500–600 words)"
    source: "book_system_intent.front_matter.preface; research 3.2 (Code Complete, Clean Code pattern)"
    validation: "First 600 words of preface.md cover problem + audience"
    test: "Word count of preface.md through end of audience section ≤ 600; both 'problem' and 'audience' topics present"

  - name: "writing_style_front_matter"
    requirement: "Preface: first person ('I') for author journey, second person ('you') for reader address. Mentor-like, direct. How to Use: second person, scannable, concise."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.voice; research 4.1 (mentor-like, direct)"
    validation: "Manual: voice matches; preface uses 'I' for journey, 'you' for reader"
    test: "Preface contains first-person pronouns ('I', 'my') in journey sections and second-person ('you', 'your') in reader-address sections; how_to_use.md uses second person throughout"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis/titles, code for paths/commands."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms on first use are bolded; book titles are italicized; file paths and commands use inline code; no ALL CAPS, no emoji, no underscore emphasis"

  - name: "author_credibility"
    requirement: "Preface must establish author credibility: experience building with AI, real projects, evidence-based approach. Pattern: McConnell (research + practice), Fowler (consulting), Uncle Bob (craft)."
    source: "research 5 (author backgrounds), 6.1 (author credibility)"
    validation: "Manual: preface includes author journey and experience anchors"
    test: "Preface contains at least one concrete experience anchor (project, company, outcome) from the author's direct work with AI"

  - name: "reading_paths"
    requirement: "How to Use must define exactly three reading paths with clear entry points and exit outcomes"
    source: "book_system_intent.front_matter.how_to_use"
    validation: "how_to_use.md contains three named paths with Part references and exit statements"
    test: "how_to_use.md contains exactly 3 named paths (Practitioner, Leader, Complete), each with Part references and a one-sentence exit outcome"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references (days, weeks, months) unless in examples"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' preface.md how_to_use.md returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' preface.md how_to_use.md returns 0 matches (excluding code examples)"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Any statistics in preface must be sourced or labeled as estimated"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "S

one-sentence exit outcome"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references (days, weeks, months) unless in examples"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' preface.md how_to_use.md returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' preface.md how_to_use.md returns 0 matches (excluding code examples)"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Any statistics in preface must be sourced or labeled as estimated"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "Statistics have inline attribution or 'estimated' label"
    test: "Every number/percentage/dollar figure in preface.md has an inline footnote, attribution, or 'estimated' label within the same sentence or paragraph"

  - name: "author_sourced_journey"
    requirement: "Author's journey in preface must be from real experience (not fabricated). Pattern: research 6.1 author-sourced examples."
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.author_sourced_examples; research 6.1"
    validation: "Manual: preface journey is authentic; no unanchored fiction"
    test: "Preface journey section contains no fictional scenarios; all claims tie to author's verifiable experience or are labeled 'anonymized'"

  - name: "no_solution_depth"
    requirement: "Front matter introduces IVD by name and promise but does not teach it. The paradigm, principles, and artifacts are taught in Part I (Chapters 1–4)."
    source: "book_system_intent.book_structure (front_matter sets up; Part I teaches)"
    validation: "No exposition of principles, artifact structure, or verification workflow; only name and promise"
    test: "rg -i '(principle [1-8]|verification workflow|bidirectional sync)' preface.md foreword.md returns 0 matches; how_to_use.md may reference 'intent artifact' and 'YAML' in structural descriptions and conventions (not teaching) — excluded from this test"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Front matter as a separate intent with three deliverables (foreword, preface, how to use). Cover & Title Page and TOC excluded (production artifacts)."

  reasoning: |
    - Research (3.2): bestselling programming books use front matter to establish problem, author
      credibility, and reading paths. Code Complete, Clean Code, and Pragmatic Programmer all have
      strong prefaces that hook the reader before Chapter 1.
    - Research (6.1): "Establish problem and who this is for in the first 2 pages" is the synthesis
      recommendation; "Author credibility" (McConnell, Fowler, Uncle Bob) builds trust.
    - Research (2.1): "Brand and credibility: author/team reputation drives trust." The preface is
      where author credibility is first established.
    - Three reading paths (book_system_intent) support different audiences without forcing sequential
      reading—pattern from Code Complete.
    - Foreword by an industry leader adds third-party credibility; placeholder until con

l have
      strong prefaces that hook the reader before Chapter 1.
    - Research (6.1): "Establish problem and who this is for in the first 2 pages" is the synthesis
      recommendation; "Author credibility" (McConnell, Fowler, Uncle Bob) builds trust.
    - Research (2.1): "Brand and credibility: author/team reputation drives trust." The preface is
      where author credibility is first established.
    - Three reading paths (book_system_intent) support different audiences without forcing sequential
      reading—pattern from Code Complete.
    - Foreword by an industry leader adds third-party credibility; placeholder until confirmed.
    - Front matter does not teach IVD; it creates the desire to learn. Chapter 1 starts the teaching.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (front_matter); research/best_selling_programming_books.md (3.2, 4.1, 5, 6.1)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Skip foreword; preface and how-to-use only"
    rejected_because: "A foreword by an industry leader adds third-party credibility that the author cannot provide alone. Research (5, 6.1): bestsellers benefit from 'by the people who invented it' or recognized-expert endorsement."
    when_it_works: "If no suitable foreword contributor is available at publication time; can be added in a later edition."

  - name: "Combine preface and how-to-use into one section"
    rejected_because: "They serve different purposes: preface = why this book (motivation, credibility); how-to-use = how to read it (paths, resources). Combining dilutes both. Pattern: Code Complete separates them."
    when_it_works: "If the book is very short (<200 pages) and minimal front matter is preferred."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Preface is too long or self-indulgent (author journey overshadows the reader's needs)"
    action: "Cap preface at ~1500 words; problem + audience in first 2 pages, then journey, then promise. Reader-first, author-second."
    monitor: "Word count; beta reader feedback: 'did the preface hook you or bore you?'"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Foreword contributor not confirmed before publication"
    action: "Publish without foreword; add in second edition. Preface stands alone."
    monitor: "Contributor outreach timeline"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Reading paths in How to Use feel prescriptive or confusing"
    action: "Keep to exactly 3 paths with clear names and 1-sentence exit outcomes. Use a table or visual map."
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'did you know which path to take?'"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Front matter gives away too much IVD detail, reducing Ch1's impact"
    action: "Constraint no_solution_depth: name IVD and state the promise, but do not teach. Chapter 1 is the first teaching content."
    monitor: "Cross-check: does the pr

ond edition. Preface stands alone."
    monitor: "Contributor outreach timeline"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Reading paths in How to Use feel prescriptive or confusing"
    action: "Keep to exactly 3 paths with clear names and 1-sentence exit outcomes. Use a table or visual map."
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'did you know which path to take?'"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Front matter gives away too much IVD detail, reducing Ch1's impact"
    action: "Constraint no_solution_depth: name IVD and state the promise, but do not teach. Chapter 1 is the first teaching content."
    monitor: "Cross-check: does the preface describe principles, artifacts, or workflow? If yes, cut."
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/front-matter/front_matter_intent.yaml"
  output:
    - "manuscript/front-matter/foreword.md"
    - "manuscript/front-matter/preface.md"
    - "manuscript/front-matter/how_to_use.md"
  status: "draft"
  version: 2

  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_5_cross_check"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_messages are conveyed across foreword, preface, and how_to_use"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "Preface establishes problem and audience within first 2 pages (~600 words)"
      validates: "constraints.problem_in_two_pages"
    - check: "Preface includes author journey grounded in real experience"
      validates: "constraints.author_credibility, constraints.author_sourced_journey"
    - check: "How to Use defines exactly 3 reading paths with entry/exit points"
      validates: "constraints.reading_paths"
    - check: "At least one quotable line in the preface"
      validates: "deliverables.manuscript.required_elements"
    - check: "Front matter names IVD but does not teach principles, artifacts, or workflow"
      validates: "constraints.no_solution_depth"
    - check: "Formatting follows conventions (bold/italic/code)"
      validates: "constraints.formatting_conventions"
    - check: "No unsourced statistics"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "No timeline references (days, weeks, months)"
      validates: "constraints.no_timeline_references"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Full cross-check (intent + ma

s: "constraints.no_solution_depth"
    - check: "Formatting follows conventions (bold/italic/code)"
      validates: "constraints.formatting_conventions"
    - check: "No unsourced statistics"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "No timeline references (days, weeks, months)"
      validates: "constraints.no_timeline_references"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Full cross-check (intent + manuscript vs master intent + research):
      (1) Fixed no_solution_depth test: was too broad — how_to_use.md must reference 'intent artifact'
          and 'YAML' per its own required_content (structural descriptions, not teaching). Test now
          scoped to preface.md and foreword.md only.
      (2) Added 'CS educators' to preface required_content and manuscript "Who Should Read" section,
          matching book_system_intent.yaml stakeholders list.
    reason: "Step 5 cross-check: 2 gaps found and resolved. All other checks pass."

  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      IVD validation + cross-check fixes:
      (1) Added `test` field to all 9 constraints (Principle 2: Understanding Must Be Executable).
      (2) Preface required_content: added "$10B+ problem" financial scale to align with
          book_system_intent.yaml front_matter.preface placeholder line ("The $10B problem").
      (3) Fixed process.current_step: was "step_3" (already completed) → "step_4_write_manuscript".
    reason: "ivd_validate returned 9 warnings (missing test field); master intent cross-check found $10B gap; process step was stale."

  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Initial front matter intent. Derived from:
      - book_system_intent.yaml front_matter (foreword, preface, how to use placeholders)
      - research/best_selling_programming_books.md (3.2 front matter patterns, 4.1 voice,
        5 author backgrounds, 6.1 synthesis: problem in 2 pages, author credibility, reading paths)
      - templates/intent.yaml (canonical structure)
      Three deliverables: foreword (placeholder for external contributor), preface (author journey
      + problem + audience + promise), how to use (3 reading paths + resources).
      Constraints: problem_in_two_pages, author_credibility, reading_paths, no_solution_depth,
      formatting, no_unsourced_statistics, author_sourced_journey.
      Risks: preface length, foreword contributor, path clarity, solution leakage.
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation. Front matter is the book's first impression; it needs the same rigor as any chapter."

sourced_journey.
      Risks: preface length, foreword contributor, path clarity, solution leakage.
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation. Front matter is the book's first impression; it needs the same rigor as any chapter."
